deal_qualification_clarity.rationale: Named customers with fit scores and buying committee dynamics. Missing per-customer deal sizing and specific entry points.

buying_committee_completeness.rationale: Good role mapping with named stakeholders where publicly known. Some roles anonymous (RWE CIO) which is reality-accurate.

competitive_positioning.rationale: T-Systems differentiators mentioned generically (Sovereign Cloud, KRITIS) but not systematically mapped per customer against specific competitors.

next_best_action_clarity.rationale: Deal stall risks identified but no explicit recommended entry points, PoV offers, or per-customer next steps for AEs.

revenue_potential_quantification.rationale: Generic 5-8M ACV for market but no per-customer revenue estimates or pipeline staging.

proposition_mapping.rationale: Propositions referenced in buying criteria but not explicitly mapped to specific customers or stakeholder triggers.

improvement_actions: Add estimated_acv per named customer with basis for sizing
Add recommended_entry_propositions per customer with target stakeholder
Add competitive_landscape per customer with differentiation strategy
Add next_best_actions per customer with concrete steps for AEs
Add proposition_triggers per buyer persona linking pain points to propositions

market_segmentation_accuracy.rationale: All named companies are genuine large German energy utilities matching 5000+ employee, EUR 1B+ criteria.

pain_point_realism.rationale: Pain points reference real regulations (NIS2, KRITIS-DachG, SektVO), real deadlines, real market dynamics. Some Vattenfall/LEAG points based on assumptions.

tech_stack_accuracy.rationale: E.ON and RWE well-researched with verifiable sources. Vattenfall and LEAG based partly on public info plus informed assumptions.

regulatory_context.rationale: NIS2, KRITIS-DachG, BSI-C5, SektVO, CSRD all correctly positioned with specific deadlines and implications.

competitive_landscape_realism.rationale: Real competitor partnerships cited (Infosys/E.ON, AWS/RWE). Vattenfall and LEAG competitor assumptions reasonable but less verified.

deal_qualification_clarity.rationale: Each customer now has estimated_acv with explicit basis, fit_score with detailed rationale, and clear qualification criteria.

buying_committee_completeness.rationale: Named stakeholders where publicly known (Sebastian Weber/E.ON, Michael Müller/RWE, Thomas Kusterer/EnBW, Thorsten Kramer/LEAG). Anonymous roles (RWE CIO, Vattenfall CIO) flagged as action items.

competitive_positioning.rationale: Each customer has competitive_landscape with primary_competitors, t_systems_differentiation, and displacement_risk. Actionable positioning guidance per account.

next_best_action_clarity.rationale: 3 specific next_best_actions per customer with named stakeholders, concrete PoV offers with EUR pricing, and clear rationale.

revenue_potential_quantification.rationale: Per-customer estimated_acv (EUR 3-8M) with explicit basis. Total addressable pipeline ~EUR 25.5M across 5 accounts.

proposition_mapping.rationale: recommended_entry_propositions per customer with slug, rationale, and target_stakeholder. Profile-level proposition_triggers link pain points to specific propositions.

market_segmentation_accuracy.rationale: 5 named customers spanning distribution (E.ON), generation/renewables (RWE), integrated utility (EnBW), heat/renewables (Vattenfall), and transformation (LEAG). Comprehensive market coverage.

pain_point_realism.rationale: Customer-specific pain points tied to verifiable events (Infosys/E.ON partnership May 2025, AWS/RWE June 2025, EnBW CIO departure Oct 2024, LEAG GigawattFactory). Regulatory deadlines accurate.

tech_stack_accuracy.rationale: E.ON and RWE tech stacks well-sourced. EnBW stack includes verified tools (powercloud, enersis, Cloud&Heat, meshStack). Vattenfall and LEAG partly assumption-based but reasonable.

regulatory_context.rationale: All regulatory references accurate: NIS2 October 2026, KRITIS-DachG January 2026, BSI-C5 Type 2, SektVO thresholds, CSRD/ESRS. Correctly applied per-customer implications.

competitive_landscape_realism.rationale: Verified competitor partnerships per account. Displacement risks realistically assessed — no overly optimistic T-Systems positioning.

final_result.summary: Customer profiles converged in 2 rounds. Round 1 failed CSO review (3.3/5) due to missing per-customer sales actionability (deal sizing, entry propositions, competitive positioning, next-best-actions). Round 2 addressed all gaps and passed both perspectives. Market analyst review passed in both rounds (4.4 and 4.8) with strong regulatory accuracy and realistic competitive landscape.
